entry.title: Re: Entering the New Era of Agentic AI for ABAP Development
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

Custom Code Agents for SAP S/4HANA Migration 

SAP S/4HANA migration projects carry significant complexity, and a large part of that complexity lies in custom ABAP code — business logic built up over many years, often undocumented and tightly coupled with SAP standard objects. Understanding it, adapting it, and validating it has historically been time consuming and expensive work.  

The Custom Code Assistant addresses this directly, bringing together a coherent, intelligent toolchain that covers all phases of an SAP S/4HANA custom code migration project. Three agents focusing on developer execution will be delivered by ABAP platform, complemented by additional agents from SAP Cloud ALM handling broader transformation governance. 

"

 

Thanks for your sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

🙂

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow – Multiple Workflow Determination f...
entry.content:
@akash_r18  I’ve done similar. You’re going to need to implement the below two BADIs.

  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_DEF
  • SWF_WORKFLOW_CONDITION_EVAL

the first BADI makes a value appear as a start condition, and the second assesses it.  You’d likely need to provide your ABAPER the CDS views that are released for key user development, as this will originate in the 100 tenant of DEV.  For example I_PRODUCT could be used for  material group, or if you allow manual overrides, both pieces of info would come from released CDS I_PurchaseOrderAPI01

entry.title: Purchase order pricing: ZCOS manual condition proposed with last historical value
entry.content:
When creating a purchase order in ME21N, custom condition type ZCOS is proposed automatically in the item Conditions tab, although ZCOS is expected to be a manual/suggested value that must be reviewed and maintained by the buyer per item.

 

Original business case: PO 4500253085 was created with different ZCOS values by item: item 10 = 37,000, item 20 = 33,000 and item 30 = 35,000. When a new PO was created for the same vendor/material context, the system proposed the last ZCOS value used in the previous PO, i.e. 35,000 from PO 4500253085 item 30.

 

Additional reproduction evidence in ME21N shows the same behavior pattern: after entering ZNAC Standard PO data, supplier 2000003633, purchasing org CA02, purchasing group 101, company code 2002, material MP13000007, quantity 20 KG and plant 2002/CAFEPAC, the Conditions tab is populated with ZCOS (Freight/Quantity) as 55.00 MXN, with a condition value of 3.09 USD.

 

Business expectation: ZCOS is not an automatic tariff/rate and should not be calculated or updated automatically. It should be entered or explicitly confirmed manually per PO item based on the specific commercial conditions applicable to

entry.title: Re: Need Process to Onboard Customer S-User ID to get acc...
entry.content:
If you want to become a Cloud Admin, the process and prerequisites are documented in the following resources:
- KBA 2596214 - How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me
- KBA 2492132 and the online help documentation for the Administrator Concept

For your convenience, I have captured screenshots of the Cloud Admin explanations from these materials for reference.

Based on my investigation, your S-user ID S0025823361 is currently missing two mandatory permissions needed to become a Cloud Admin: Display Cloud Data and Edit Cloud Data. Because these permissions are not present, your S-user is not a Cloud Admin at the moment.

Please kindly note that SAP applies strict user management policies. I am not authorized to assign the Cloud Admin role to your S-user if your company already has an active Cloud Admin. This safeguard is designed to protect your company’s data security.

Next steps:

please contact your company’s Cloud Admin and ask them to assign the Cloud Admin role to you. They can follow the step-by-step guidance in the KBAs below. 
Cloud: Super Administrator

1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me

1614304 - How to use the Copy Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Manage Customer Line Items – Net Days and WBS Element...
entry.content:
Hi Pablo,

Thank you for raising this question. I would like to share the following findings that may help clarify the behavior you observed.

1. Days Net Field

The Days Net field corresponds to the field BSEG-ZBD3T, which is derived from the payment terms configuration in SSCUI 102934 - Maintain Terms of Payment. Based on my testing in an internal system, the field is populated correctly once the relevant settings are properly maintained in SSCUI 102934. I would recommend verifying your payment terms configuration there. See KBA  3774079 - Days Net field displays 0 in Manage Customer Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me for more details.

2. WBS Element Field

The Manage Customer Line Items app displays line items from a financial operations perspective, showing invoiced amounts, due dates, and dunning status. Information related to WBS elements, profit centers, and segments belongs to G/L Accounting and is not displayed in this app by design. To view WBS element information, please use the Display Line Items in General Ledger app instead. For further details, please refer to KBA 3346815 - Profit Center not displayed in Manage Supplier Line Items and Manage Customer Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me and Manage Customer Line Items: FAQ.

I hope the above information is helpful. Feel free to reach out if you have any further questions.

Best regards,
Gordon

entry.title: Re: Unable to delete S-User ID from the User List in SAP...
entry.content:
I understand you have an issue that could not delete S-user*.

I reviewed our backend and confirmed that S-user * cannot be deleted because it is assigned as a Partner Contact. For background and guidance on Partner Contacts, please see KBA 1271517 - Important Contact descriptions and how to view/change S-users with important functions - SAP for Me. 

However the authorizations required for Partner Dashboards are different than Customer Dashboards. We here at SAP Support do not have relevant authorization to support SAP for Me Partner Dashboards or any of the related partner cards.

To resolve your access problem, please refer to the steps and attached screenshots below:

  • Navigate to the designated URL： https://partneredge.sap.com/en/welcome.html
  • Click Get Support to reach out to the dedicated Partner Support team for assistance.

Additionally, I would like to clarify the process handling for this case. This is an SAE case rather than a standard support case. You have booked a 30-minute SAE session as below, and per internal process, I am required to join the session to discuss the issue with you during the meeting.

Appointment has been scheduled on *.

Subject: Unable to delete S-User ID from the User List in SAP for Me

However, our team does not possess the authorization to resolve partner-related inquiries. If I attend this scheduled session, it will only delay the resolution of your issue.

For this reason, I have shared the correct resolution steps with you ahead of the session start time.

After reviewing the guidance, you may follow the proper procedure to reach out to the Partner Support team for dedicated assistance.

entry.title: Re: Issue Excel file Update users roles
entry.content:
Hi GDB1,

It should work if you follow the correct steps.  Please try the following:

1. Open Manager
2. Select Administration > Users
3. Click the Export button - bottom, left side
4. Modify the Source of the user records, if relevant and click Export
5. Open the generated Excel Workbook
6. Update the Position column value for each user to indicate their Roles.  Roles should be separated by commas with no spaces. Do not remove the heading entries in row 1
7. Save the change to the Workbook and close it
8. In Manager, select Administration > User Import
9. Click Excel, then click Choose File
10. Locate the file and click Import Excel File

If these steps don't work, please provide more information about the error and I will try to determine what is going wrong.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: SAP PO 7.5 REST Receiver - Capture HTTP 500 response in Asynchronous Interface and send via alert
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an asynchronous interface in SAP PO 7.5 from SAP to D365(proxy to rest) using the REST Receiver Adapter.

When the target REST API (D365) returns HTTP 500, the response contains a useful JSON error like this:

{
"Message": "Receipt processing failed for Purchase Order "5*********"
}
I can see this response in the Message Monitor under the server response, but it is not available as the XI message payload.

My requirement is to automatically send this business error to users by email instead of manually copying it from Message Monitor.

My questions are:

Can SAP PO capture the HTTP 500 response body in a standard asynchronous REST receiver?
Can the REST Result Handler be used to extract the Message field and use it for email notifications?
If not, what is the SAP-recommended approach?

entry.title: Custom Root Entity Use case along with custom messages in RAP
entry.content:
A Custom Entity

A Custom Entity has no real table linked to it. RAP cannot read it automatically. Instead, it creates an empty structure and hands over the control to you. You then write your own ABAP code inside a Query Provider Class (QPC).

Custom Root Entity Example

@EndUserText.label: 'Custom Root Entity'

@ObjectModel: {
  query: {
    implementedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_SALES_STATUS'
  }
}

@UI: {
  headerInfo: {
    typeName: 'SalesData',
    typeNamePlural: 'SalesData',
    title: { value: 'Salesorder' },
    description: { value: 'Itemno' }
  }
}

define root custom entity ZCR_CUSTSO_VIEW
{

  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 , label: 'Sales Order' }]
  @Consumption.filter.mandatory: true
  @Search.defaultSearchElement: true
  @UI.identification:[{ position: 10 , label: 'Sales Order'}]
  key Salesorder : vbeln_va;

  @Consumption.filter.mandatory: true
  @Search.defaultSearchElement: true
  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 , label: 'Sales Order Item' }]
  @UI.identification: [{ position: 20 , label: 'Sales Order Item' }]
  key Itemno : posnr_va;

  @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
    entity: {
      name: 'ZStatus_VH', // Your Value Help View
      element: 'Value'    // Source field for Status
    },
    additionalBinding: [{
      localElement: 'Statusdesc',   // Target field in your app
      element: 'Description',       // Source field from Value Help View
      usage: #RESULT                // Passes data on selection
    }]
  }]

  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30 , label: 'Sales Order Status' }]
  @UI.identification: [{ position: 30 , label: 'Sales Order Status' }]
  Status : abap.char( 1 );

  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40 , label: 'Time Stamp' }]
  @UI.identification: [{ position: 40 , label: 'Time Stamp' }]
  Timestamp : timestampl;

  @UI.identification: [{ position: 50 , label: 'Status Description' }]
  @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 50 , label: 'Status Description' }]
  Statusdesc : abap.char(20);

}

As you have created a Custom Root Entity, you are not forced to fetch data from a database source.

You can validate the input before fetching the data, or you can write your validation logic along with the data retrieval because you are implementing the query yourself.

You can loop over the data, modify it, apply your own business logic, or perform any operation you require before returning the response.

CLASS zcl_sales_status DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

PUBLIC SECTION.

  INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider .

PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcl_sales_status IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.

  DATA(lv_top) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).

  IF lv_top < 0.
    lv_top = 1.
  ENDIF.

  DATA(lv_skip) = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).

  DATA(lt_sort) = io_request->get_sort_elements( ).

  DATA : lv_orderby TYPE string.

  LOOP AT lt_sort INTO DATA(ls_sort).

    IF ls_sort-descending = abap_true.
      lv_orderby = |'{ lv_orderby } { ls_sort-element_name } DESCENDING |.
    ELSE.
      lv_orderby = |'{ lv_orderby } { ls_sort-element_name } ASCENDING |.
    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

  IF lv_orderby IS INITIAL.
    lv_orderby = 'Salesorder'.
  ENDIF.

  DATA(lv_conditions) = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_sql_string( ).

  SELECT FROM zebst_status_log
    FIELDS salesorder,
           itemno,
           status,
           statusdesc,
           timestamp
    WHERE (lv_conditions)
    ORDER BY (lv_orderby)
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_status)
    UP TO @lv_top ROWS
    OFFSET @lv_skip.

  IF lt_status IS INITIAL.

    IF lv_conditions IS NOT INITIAL.

      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_rap_exception_provider
        EXPORTING
          textid = VALUE scx_t100key(

*           HOW TO ADD CUSTOM MESSAGE CREATED IN SE91 (ZMSG_STATUS)

            msgid = 'ZMSG_STATUS'
            msgno = '000'
          )
          previous = NEW cx_sadl_contract_violation( ).

    ELSEIF lv_conditions IS INITIAL.

      RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE zcx_rap_exception_provider
        EXPORTING
          textid = VALUE scx_t100key(

*           HOW TO ADD CUSTOM MESSAGE CREATED IN SE91 (ZMSG_STATUS)

            msgid = 'ZMSG_STATUS'
            msgno = '001'
          )
          previous = NEW cx_sadl_contract_violation( ).

    ENDIF.

  ELSE.

    IF io_request->is_total_numb_of_rec_requested( ).
      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_status ) ).
      io_response->set_data( lt_status ).
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

Using this approach, you can display a custom message when the user clicks the Go button.

In Managed and Unmanaged RAP scenarios, it is not straightforward to display this type of validation message during the read operation because the framework handles the data retrieval automatically. However, with a Custom Root Entity and Query Provider Class, you have complete control over the query execution and can perform validations before returning the data.

Custom Exception Class

To display custom messages, you need to create a custom exception class by inheriting from the standard RAP Query Provider exception class.

CLASS zcx_rap_exception_provider DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  INHERITING FROM cx_rap_query_provider
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

PUBLIC SECTION.

  METHODS constructor
    IMPORTING
      !textid   LIKE if_t100_message=>t100key OPTIONAL
      !previous LIKE previous OPTIONAL.

PROTECTED SECTION.
PRIVATE SECTION.

ENDCLASS.

CLASS zcx_rap_exception_provider IMPLEMENTATION.

METHOD constructor ##ADT_SUPPRESS_GENERATION.

  CALL METHOD super->constructor
    EXPORTING
      previous = previous.

  CLEAR me->textid.

  IF textid IS INITIAL.
    if_t100_message~t100key = if_t100_message=>default_textid.
  ELSE.
    if_t100_message~t100key = textid.
  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

After creating this custom exception class, you can use your custom SE91 message class (for example, ZMSG_STATUS) to display meaningful validation messages directly in the Fiori application when the user clicks the Go button.

This approach gives you complete control over the read operation. You can validate user input, fetch data from any source, modify the retrieved data, implement custom business logic, and raise custom exceptions whenever required.

entry.title: Cant download complete log from COFC

Dear Team ,

While performing mass result recording using the F1685A – Record Inspection Results app, the process completes successfully without any errors.

However, when attempting to record results in mass using the F6607 – Manage Inspection Checklists app, the following issues are observed:

entry.title: Re: KSII - Maessage KD301
entry.content:
The issue arises because the confirmation of activities is being done in seconds, while the activity type master data is maintained in hours. Although the conversion for cost valuation is working correctly, the allocation cycles and actual cost rate determination rely on activity quantities in hours. Since the system does not find the hours-based quantities, the tracing factor becomes zero, leading to error KD301.

There is one SAP Note 1962182 which is indeed relevant for ECC, but since you are in S/4HANA, the note does not directly apply. In S/4, the recommendation is to ensure that:

  • The unit of measure for activity confirmation aligns with the unit of measure defined in the activity type master data.
  • Conversion factors between seconds and hours are consistently maintained in customizing (check CUNI transaction).
  • Splitting rules and assignment (MOD) are reviewed to confirm that tracing factors are derived correctly from actual activity quantities.

Having said that, the next step is please check whether the activity type master data in S/4 has the correct unit of measure (hours) and whether confirmations can be adjusted or converted consistently. If the mismatch persists, it may require raising an OSS incident with SAP Support referencing this scenario, since the ECC note does not cover S/4 functionality.

entry.title: Installation Query for Ariba R/3 in SAP for ME
entry.content:
We received a notification regarding an installation update on 18 June. Upon checking SAP for Me, it appears to relate to an Ariba R/3 installation (please see the attached screenshots).

Could someone please advise what this installation relates to and what or who triggered it on 18 June? We were not aware of any internal activities or requests that would have initiated this installation.

Thanks in advance for your assistance. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Installation Query for Ariba R/3 in SAP for ME
entry.content:
I understand you want to know why installation 91105348 was created.

Based on my work experience, the creation of Cloud installations is typically tied to the creation or update of contracts. This points to a contractual process rather than a technical defect.

To move this forward, please refer to the relevant KBA 3259711 - Who to contact at SAP for contract and/or licensing questions - SAP for Me and contact theSAP Order Management (contracts) by e-mailing sapcontractcenter@sap.com 

  • Contact SAP Order Management (contracts) by e-mailing sapcontractcenter@sap.com according to KBA 1660069 for help with the following licensing/contract topics:

    .... need help updating the contract or maintenance for an installation number
    .... transferring contracts between customer numbers, migrating contracts, merging customers
    .... check on the status of a new contract (and new installations)
    .... missing contractual assigned product licenses
    .... request to assign or extend a maintenance frame, fix expired maintenance, see KBA 2436412
    .... request for information on a contract volume status
    .... partner / end user customer account changes 
    .... updating the partner responsible for an end customer's installation numbers, see KBA 2929126
    .... need to register a database license purchased through database vendor
    .... request for new or updated Hardware Data, see KBA 2745518
    .... request help creating, reactivating, or deleting installation numbers
    .... need to terminate cloud contracts/orders
    .... clarification on S-user creation reason (contract-related) 

I also recommend involving your Account Manager, as they usually have the most complete view of your company’s contractual setup and can help clarify the action that triggered this installation.

entry.title: Re: Storage location population in sales order
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

To autopopulate the storage location at the sales order item level, you can consider using the Storage Location Substitution feature in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This feature allows you to define substitution strategies that can automatically determine alternative storage locations when the specified location cannot fulfill the order. This process is described in the following SAP Help Portal document: Storage Location Substitution.

Steps to Implement Storage Location Substitution:

  1. Configure Substitution Strategy: Use the ‘Configure Substitution Strategy’ app to define a substitution strategy for the method ‘Storage Location Substitution’. For more information about the app, see Configure Substitution Strategy,

  2. Assign Substitution Strategy: Assign the substitution strategy to characteristic value combinations in the ‘Configure Alternative Control’ app. This defines for which sales order requirements the substitution strategy is valid and which alternative storage locations are to be considered. For more information about the app, see Configure Alternative Control.

  3. Define Master Data: Use the ‘Manage Substitutions - Locations’ app to define substitution master data for storage locations. This involves specifying which storage location can be substituted by another. For more information about the app, see Manage Substitutions.

  4. Process Execution: During the availability check, the system will automatically consider the defined substitutions and populate the storage location based on the substitution strategy.

This approach ensures that the storage location is automatically populated based on predefined rules, improving efficiency and accuracy in order processing.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Tax and Pricing on Purchase Order
entry.content:
#GenAI-Assisted Content

Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. 

I asked our internal chatbot for support, and here's the response. Please check whether the suggestions help you resolve your issue:

1. Setting Default Taxes Without Purchasing Info Record

Yes, you can set taxes to appear automatically without using a Purchasing Info Record. This can be achieved by configuring the system to determine tax codes based on pricing conditions. You can create condition records for tax rates using the ‘Set Tax Rates - Purchasing’ app, which allows the system to automatically determine the tax rate based on the supplier, country/region, and tax classification of the materials. This information is stored as master data in the form of condition records. For more information about the app, see Set Tax Rates - Purchasing app.

 

2. Ensuring Condition Type TTX1 Appears in Pricing

To ensure that the condition type TTX1 always appears in pricing, you need to configure the system to include this condition type in the pricing procedure. You can modify the standard configuration or create a custom configuration based on your business  requirements. The procedure involves creating a custom condition table, access sequence, pricing condition type, and calculation schema. Assign the condition type TTX1 to the calculation schema used for purchase orders. For more information, see Determining Tax Code in Purchase Documents using Pricing Conditions.

For detailed steps, you can refer to the configuration activities such as ‘Create Condition Table’, (Custom Fields: Sales Document), ‘Define Access Sequences for Purchasing’, ‘Set Pricing Condition Types - Purchasing’, and ‘Set Calculation Schema - Purchasing’.

 

By following these steps, the system will check the field values for line items in a purchase order and determine the tax codes based on the condition type configuration.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Request for Workaround on Intercompany Returns Credit...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

Here's what the expert replied:

  • It is not possible to support intercompany refund – credit memo from delivering company and credit memo verification at selling company.
  • Also, it is not possible to support intercompany returns under the Lean return solution.

If the process can be reversed, that would be better.

To deal with intercompany refund, I can only see two options,

  1. Delivering company & selling company - both sides can post FI document directly with right account.
  2. Or Deliver company manually create Credit Memo Request (document type DR, without reference)  and credit memo 

Selling company -manually post FI document with right account.

I hope this helps.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Cash Flow Statement (Direct Method) - Problem
entry.content:
Good day @6-Mar 

Thank you for contacting via mail on this topic. Here is my addition to the links provided earlier by @HenrikeGroetecke.

I think the first you should note is that a supplier payment posted successfully through Post Outgoing Payments is not automatically guaranteed to appear in the direct method Cash Flow Statement.                                              

The report classifies the payment only when the payment’s cash account and the determined offsetting or invoice account match the account combination maintained for the Supplier payments reporting item. Note that debit or credit transaction items require at least one cash account and one offsetting account in the cash flow statement version. Therefore, maintaining only the supplier reconciliation account is not sufficient in this case.

This also explains why @ HenrikeGroetecke forwarded those links to you.

See Example of Cash Flow Statement Version Settings for more details.

You should perform the following checks:

  1. In Manage Journal Entries, check the company code, posting date, ledger, supplier reconciliation account, bank or bank clearing account and the cleared supplier invoice. Make sure the posting date falls within the report period and the document is not reversed.    
  2. Open Manage Cash Flow Statement Version, select the exact version entered in the statutory report and choose the Supplier payments item. Confirm that your configuration contains the following:
    1. Cash Account: the bank, cash or clearing account used in the payment
    2. Offsetting Account: the account determined against the cash line
    3. Invoice Account: the original expense or asset account, where invoice account logic is used
  3. Verify that online offsetting account determination is active. For supported countries and regions. If your documents were posted before activation, process them using Determine Offsetting Accounts for Unprocessed Journal Entries. See KBA 3198141 for more details.
  4. Then regenerate the report in Run Statutory Reports with the same company code, ledger, period and cash flow statement version. See Generic Cash Flow Statements for more insights.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Flexible Workflow – Multiple Workflow Determination f...
entry.content:
Dear @akash_r18,

We can use the "Manage Teams and Responsibilities" fiori, where we can create a 2 teams 
where we create one team for each approval group with required responsibility definition.
Ex: Team 1 or Approval group 1 which has the Approver 1 and Approver 2 in the team memebers with Function = custom function or standard function  and reponsibility definitions for this team as Purchasing Group  = VMU
Another team for Approval Group 2, which has the Aprover 3 and 4 in the team memebers with Function for team members = different one and reponsibility definition as Material group = required material group 

In the workflow steps, create one step for each team and in the assign to the required function with required step condition.
So that the workflow for team 1 will be send to team 1 members only if reponsibility definition match (Purchasing group = VMU) and for team 2 members only if its material group matched.

If not we can use only the function with steps conditions to match the requirements.

Hope this helps!!!

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Enter G/l account error when sending Contract line item document to S4 from Ariba Contract workspace
entry.content: When sending Contract Line item document having Material Master added from ariba Contract using send to external system to S4 received the error "Enter G/l account". Upon checking the material type of Material master in Ariba and mara table in S4, it is not same. Material type of Material master mismatch between the Ariba and S4 is causing the issue. To resolve, we may check whether the integration for material master imports changes to material type in S4 to Ariba through Standard CIG integration or send Material master with updated Material type to Ariba, then resend the CLID to S4

entry.title: Enterprise Service Organization: Determination of Service Team using Member Functions
entry.content:
In an enterprise service model enabled system, a service team is an organizational unit to which service employees are assigned to perform service tasks. It facilitates the assignment of service employees in service transactions, related analytics, and the search for service transactions.­­ 

For information related to service teams, please check out- 

Service Teams | SAP Help Portal 

How to Use Service Teams in Service Transactions - SAP Community  

Enterprise Service Organization: Determination ... - SAP Community 

 A service team consists of attributes, team members, and team owners. Service team can be searched on the basis of attributes and team members.  

 From release SAP S/4 HANA OP 2025 FPS1, Service team determination can be done using 3 ways: 

  1. Service team determination using member (e.g. - employee responsible) 
  2. Service team determination using executing service employee
  3. Service team determination using the ­­­standard and custom attributes 

 Service team determination using member and attributes is supported only on header level. Service team determination using executing service employee is supported on both header and item level.  

  1. Service team determination using member function:  

Service team determination can be done using member functions by mapping team function to corresponding partner function in - Service -> Master Data -> Service Teams -> Define Service Team Determination Rules -> Assign Functions  

When an employee responsible is entered, based on this partner and its corresponding team function, the team is determined by checking if it contains a member which matches with partner ID and mapped function. 

For example, if BP 9980000191 is entered, then the team is determined based on partner function - business partner and team function.   

Above screenshot is from Create Service Order App 

 

Above screenshot is from Manage Teams and Responsibilities App  

To define team function, go to Define function in SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Cross-Application Components -> General Application Functions -> Responsibility Management -> Functions -> Define Functions  

Assign the function created in previous step to the profile ‘SERVICE’ using IMG Path: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Cross-Application Components -> General Application Functions -> Responsibility Management -> Functions -> Define Function Profiles 

 

Assign the function and function classification to the team category ‘SERVICE’ using IMG Path: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Cross-Application Components -> General Application Functions -> Responsibility Management -> Teams and Responsibilities -> Team Categories -> Classify Functions for Team Category 

 2. Service team determination using executing service employee  

The event callback function module CRMS4_SRVTEAM_FROM_PARTNER_EC is responsible for service team determination using executing service employee.  

Above screenshot is from T-code CRMV_EVENT. 

When an executing service employee is entered, based on this partner, the team is determined.

Above screenshot is from Create Service Order App  

In case the service team determination is to be done based on executing service employee mentioned in point 1, then the service team determination based on event callback needs to be disabled from Service -> Master Data -> Service Teams -> Define Service Team Profile and Assign Service Team Types.  

Because the service team determination based on event callback will take the highest priority and overwrite service team determination using member function – executing service employee. 

This determination has the highest priority over other sources of service team. Even if the service team was manually entered by the user, the team determined by this technique will overwrite the manual entry. 

Check the checkbox “Disable Exec Srv Emp’ to disable the service team determination using this technique. 

 

3. Service team determination using attributes  

Service team determination can be done using attributes by maintaining responsibility definition, source, and partner function in - Service -> Master Data -> Service Teams -> Define Service Team Determination Rules -> Assign Attributes 

 

The service team determination process considers the standard and custom attributes in the Enterprise org model enabled system. The determination rule can be built with both standard and custom attributes.   

For example, when a service order is created, and a sold-to-party is added, based on this partner and its corresponding attribute, the team is determined by checking if that attribute is maintained for a team. 

Above screenshot is from Create Service Order App  

The users can make use of any of the attributes available on the service team (standard and custom attributes) in responsibility management definition to determine the Service team. 

 

Above screenshot is from Manage Teams and Responsibilities App  

The value source can be enhanced using a source BAdI so that, in the customization dropdown, a third option is available alongside Organizational Data and BP. 

 

Using the BAdI, the customer can implement their own logic to find the value for both custom and standard attributes.  

BAdI Definition Name: CRMS4_RM_SRVTEAM_DETERMINATION  

BAdI Interface: IF_CRMS4_RM_SRVTEAM_DET  

Method: READ_ATTRIBUTE_VALUE 

Method Signature: 

Importing IV_REF_GUID type CRMT_OBJECT_GUID 

    IV_REF_KIND type CRMT_OBJECT_KIND  

    IS_DET_CONDITION type CRMS4C_TEAM_CDAT  

 Changing CT_ATTRIBUTE_VALUE type CL_CRMS4_ORGMAN_DET_SRV_TEAM=>GTYT_ATTRIBUTE_VALUES  

To enable service team determination based on segment data changes, the EC function module CRMS4_SRVTEAM_DETERM_REG_EC must be registered. 

SAP provides event registration for the following objects: 

  • ORGMAN (After Create, After Change) 
  • PARTNER (After Create, After Change, After Delete)  

If a customer needs to enable service team determination for a different object event, they must register it against the event callback function module CRMS4_SRVTEAM_DETERM_REG_EC.  

For more information on determination of Service Team via Standard and Custom attributes, please check out this blog - Enterprise Service Organization: Determination ... - SAP Community  

Define Condition and Determination Rules: 

A determination rule can contain conditions in a step-by-step scenario. Conditions can be assigned with attributes and/or member functions for service team determination. Based on step numbers, consecutive service team determination will occur based on assigned conditions.  

For any condition, if both the attributes and functions are maintained, then common teams will be considered for final determination.   

  • Conclusive indicator: 

Conclusive Indicator keeps the determination flexible, that is, based on this checkbox, restrict the determination to execute for all the configured conditions.   

The system determines the results from the conditions sequentially. The system will stop determination when it reaches the condition for which the conclusive indicator has been enabled and will accumulate all the results until this step.  

However, if no results have been determined, the system will continue evaluating the subsequent conditions irrespective of the conclusive indicator being enabled.  

If the conclusive indicator is not selected, the system will evaluate all conditions and will accumulate all the results. 

 

The following table explains the functionality of conclusive indicator based on data from above screenshot:  

Scenario 

Results if Service Team is derived 

Results if Service Team is not derived 

When Employee Responsible is marked as exclusive 

Determine service team based on employee responsible only 

Determine service team based on sold-to-party and executing service employee 

When Sold-to-Party is marked as exclusive 

Determine service team based on sold-to-party and employee responsible only 

Determine service team based on executing service employee 

When Executing Service Employee is marked as exclusive 

Determine service team based on sold-to-party, employee responsible and executing service employee 

No results  

 Once the determination rule is maintained, assign it to service team profile at Service -> Master Data -> Service Teams -> Define Service Team Profile and Assign Service Team Types.  

Conclusion: 

Service Team Determination in SAP S/4HANA OP 2025 FPS1 provides flexible and powerful methods to determine service teams based on business needs.    

  • Multiple Determination Approaches: You can determine service teams using team members, executing service employees, and both standard and custom attributes, giving you broad adaptability in your service organization. 
  • Header and Item Level Control: Member and attribute-based determination is supported at the header level, while executing service employee-based determination is available at both header and item levels, allowing for granular assignment. 
  • Configuration and Customization: You can map team functions to partner functions, organize determination rules, and use conclusive indicators to control the logic flow for precise team assignment. 
  • Enhancement Capabilities: The BAdI CRMS4_RM_SRVTEAM_DETERMINATION enables you to adjust determination logic, supporting both standard and custom scenarios. 
  • Organizational Clarity: By structuring teams, attributes, and rules, your service organization becomes more searchable, assignable, and analytically robust.  

For detailed implementation guidance and technical specifications, please consult the official SAP help documentation  and engage with your SAP implementation partner to ensure an optimal configuration for your specific business needs.

entry.title: Re: Import button not showing in the display dimension APP
entry.content:
hello, 

For details on replication of unit of measures and dimensions, refer Code List Replication.

The Import option is only available if at least one unit of measure was previously created through file import. If all units of measure were created through data replication, the import option is disabled. You need not perform import dimensions or import unit of measures.

Regards,
Pravesh

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public WM – How to group multiple p...
entry.content:
Good day @Gabriel_Salomez 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your scenario, the Public Cloud does not merge warehouse tasks with different source storage bins into one warehouse task. Each source bin movement must remain separately traceable.

However, the standard solution is for you to group the warehouse tasks into one warehouse order. The warehouse order acts as one work package for the operator, while the individual warehouse tasks remain visible and are confirmed separately. See Warehouse Order Creation for more details.

So, in simple terms:

  • You cannot turn ten different source bin movements into one warehouse task.
  • You can group the ten tasks into one warehouse order.
  • You can sort the tasks according to the preferred warehouse travel sequence.
  • The operator can process the warehouse order through RF or using one printed pick list.
  • You can use a pick handling unit to collect the products during the same warehouse trip.

I recommend you first check whether the ten warehouse tasks are already assigned to one warehouse order.

Open Process Warehouse Tasks-Picking,  

filter for the ten affected warehouse tasks, open the task details and check the following values:

  • Warehouse order number
  • Source storage bin
  • Destination storage bin
  • Warehouse task status

All ten warehouse tasks should have the same warehouse order number where the configured warehouse order creation rule and limit permit this.

If different warehouse order numbers are displayed, ask the warehouse config expert to review the following configuration acitivities in the configuration environment:

  1. Define how the tasks should be ordered for warehouse execution.
  2. Assign the available sort fields required for the warehouse travel sequence.
  3. Check that the maximum number of tasks and any weight, volume, or processing limits do not split the ten tasks into separate warehouse orders.
  4. Confirm that the correct sort rule, filter and limit values are included in the applicable creation rule.
  5. Confirm that the rule you assigned to the activity area are used by the affected source bins.

Those steps are provided in the link below.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/e12ec3b67c6a4ad694904419e4edae37.html?locale=en-US

After you are done with checking the configuration, create a fresh test set of ten warehouse task. The task should remain separate, but the system should assign them to one warehouse order and sorts them according to the sequence you configured.

The operator can then process the warehouse order through the RF picking transaction.

The printed Pick List can also show the warehouse order, individual tasks, source bins, destination bins, products and quantities.

Combined Picking is an additional option, but it only combines RF picking tasks with matching attributes. It should not be treated as a general method for merging tasks from different source bins.

Waves can coordinate and release warehouse activities together, but they do not replace warehouse order creation or merge the physical movements. See Processing of Waves for mor details.

In summary:

The tasks cannot be merged into one warehouse task because the source bins are different. The recommended standard approach is for you to group and sort them within one warehouse order and execute the order through RF or a pick list. If the tasks are still split, review the creation rule, activity area assignment, sort rule, and active warehouse order limits.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Planning: Where AI meets strategy and execution

SAP Sapphire introduced a new vision for Enterprise Planning, and it marks an important step forward for organizations looking to connect strategy with execution through AI.

If you work with SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning or SAP Business Data Cloud, this webinar will help you understand where these solutions are heading and why that matters for your customers. You'll see how AI agents, trusted business data, and managed business content come together to simplify planning, accelerate decision making, and create a more connected planning experience.

The thing is, planning has become much more than creating budgets or forecasts. It now means responding faster to change while keeping finance, operations, and strategy aligned.

Join us to explore SAP's roadmap for Enterprise Planning, discover the innovations announced at SAP Sapphire, and learn how today's SAP SAC and SAP Business Data Cloud investments are shaping the next generation of intelligent planning.

entry.title: 从 Assistant 到 Agent：理解 SAP 新一代代理式 AI 创新
entry.content:
AP Sapphire 2026 进一步明确了 SAP Business AI 的演进方向：AI 不再只是嵌入在单个应用中的辅助功能，而是逐步走向面向业务流程的 Assistant 与 Agent 协同模式。

在这一模式下，用户可以通过自然语言表达业务目标，系统基于角色、流程上下文和可信业务数据，协调不同的 AI Agent 执行任务，并在必要时触发确定性的系统动作。SAP Cloud ERP 的智能能力也因此从“单点辅助”进一步演进为更具情境感知、任务关联和业务导向性的代理式 AI 能力。

本文将围绕 SAP 新一代 Assistant、Agent 与 Skill 的概念展开，并结合采购寻源场景，帮助客户和合作伙伴理解这一能力如何支持 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中的业务流程创新。

Assistant、Agent 与 Skill 的关系

在 SAP 新一代 AI 体验中，Assistant、Agent 和 Skill 分别承担不同职责。

Assistant 是面向业务用户的入口。
它利用用户角色和流程上下文，理解用户提出的业务意图，并协调相关 Agent 执行跨业务的复杂工作流。Assistant 更像一位智能团队成员，帮助用户把业务目标拆解为可以执行的任务。

Agent 是面向特定目标构建的 AI 代理。
它具备深厚的业务流程专业知识，可通过 Assistant 访问，并自动化处理定义明确的多步骤企业工作流。Agent 的重点不是回答问题，而是围绕具体业务目标执行任务、分析信息并提出行动建议。

Skill 则是更专业化、原子化和确定性的动作能力。
例如检索业务数据、调用规则、触发系统事务或执行预定义步骤。Skill 使 Agent 能够在安全、可控的边界内完成具体动作。

简单来说：

  • Assistant 负责理解意图和协调工作
  • Agent 负责完成面向业务目标的任务
  • Skill 负责执行确定性的系统动作

这也是 SAP 代理式 AI 与通用聊天式 AI 的重要区别：它不是简单地生成文本，而是面向企业业务流程完成工作。

示例：寻源助手如何协调多个 Agent

 

以上图中的采购寻源场景为例，用户不再需要分别进入多个应用，手工查找供应商、准备 RFx、比较投标并整理谈判策略，而是可以通过一个“寻源助手”表达业务目标。

例如，用户可以提出：

“帮我为这个新品类找到合适供应商，并准备一轮 RFx。”

寻源助手首先捕获用户意图，理解当前采购品类、预算、合规要求、供应商历史表现和业务优先级。随后，它会协调多个专门 Agent 分工处理。

供应商发现 Agent
基于品类、地区、风险、交付能力和历史绩效等维度识别潜在供应商，帮助采购团队更快形成候选供应商清单，并保留推荐理由。

RFx 编排 Agent
辅助生成或完善 RFx 内容，包括问题清单、报价结构、交付要求和评估标准，减少采购人员从零准备文档的工作量。

谈判智能 Agent
结合历史采购价格、市场趋势、供应商表现和合同条款，给出谈判策略和风险提示，帮助采购人员更有准备地进入谈判环节。

投标分析 Agent
对供应商响应进行比较，识别价格差异、条款风险、交付承诺和异常报价，辅助采购团队做出更清晰的决策。

在这些 Agent 之下，Skill 负责执行更具体、可控的系统动作，例如读取供应商主数据、检索合同条款、调用价格信息、创建 RFx 草稿、更新状态或触发审批流程。

这个例子体现了 Assistant 与 Agent 的核心价值：
用户不再被迫手工拆解流程、切换应用和串联任务，而是由 Assistant 理解业务目标，再由多个 Agent 协同完成复杂流程。

为什么 SAP Agent 不等同于通用 AI Agent

当前市场上有很多关于 AI Agent 的讨论，但 SAP 的 Agent 有非常明确的企业级边界。

在财务、采购、制造、销售和供应链等核心流程中，AI 不能只给出“看起来合理”的答案。它需要基于可信业务数据、标准流程、用户权限和可审计机制运行。

因此，SAP 的 Agent 能力强调以下几个基础：

业务流程基础
Agent 基于 SAP 长期沉淀的端到端业务流程和行业最佳实践，而不是孤立的任务自动化。

数据语义基础
Agent 需要理解企业业务对象、流程关系和上下文信息。SAP Business Data Cloud、SAP Knowledge Graph 等能力将为代理式 AI 提供更可信的数据和语义基础。

治理与权限基础
Agent 的执行需要遵循用户权限、业务规则、审计要求和合规边界。对于关键业务流程，human-in-control 仍然是重要设计原则。

云 ERP 与 clean core 基础
标准化流程、clean core 架构和持续创新机制，是客户持续吸收 SAP Business AI 创新的基础。

这也是 SAP Business AI 的差异化所在：AI 能力不是脱离系统运行的外部工具，而是嵌入在 SAP 应用、数据和流程治理体系中。

从 AI Feature 到端到端流程协同

过去，SAP Cloud ERP 中的 AI 创新更多体现在单点功能中，例如 AI 辅助错误解释、字段推荐、业务见解生成、文档信息提取或智能建议。这些能力已经显著提升了用户效率。

而 Assistant 与 Agent 的新范式，则进一步把 AI 能力组织到端到端流程中。

例如：

Source to Pay
支持寻源、RFx、合同、采购申请、供应商确认、发票校验和付款建议等流程环节。

Lead to Cash
支持线索识别、销售机会推进、报价、订单处理、开票、收款和争议处理等场景。

Record to Report
支持日记账、对账、差异分析、错误处理、关账、现金洞察和财务分析等任务。

Supply Chain Management
支持需求计划、生产计划、库存调整、物流执行、生产订单检查和异常响应等场景。

这意味着 AI 的价值不再只是提升某个操作步骤的效率，而是帮助企业缩短从业务信号到行动执行的时间。

对 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户的启示

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户来说，Assistant 和 Agent 的落地并不是孤立的新功能启用，而是与云 ERP 标准化、业务数据质量和流程治理能力密切相关。

客户可以从以下几个方面提前准备：

识别高价值业务场景
优先关注高频、重复、跨步骤、数据充分且业务价值清晰的流程，例如采购寻源、订单异常处理、关账、现金管理、生产订单检查等。

明确自动化边界
区分哪些任务可以由 Agent 自动完成，哪些任务需要用户确认，哪些任务必须经过审批或审计。

完善数据和权限基础
Agent 的建议质量依赖于业务数据质量，执行能力依赖于清晰的权限模型和组织职责设计。

坚持 clean core 原则
越标准、越可升级的系统架构，越容易持续吸收 SAP 后续发布的 Business AI 创新。

用业务结果衡量价值
例如流程周期是否缩短，人工处理工作量是否下降，异常处理是否加快，合规风险是否降低。

获取最新 AI Feature 信息

SAP Business AI 相关能力更新较快，建议客户和合作伙伴定期访问 SAP Business AI Feature Catalog，按产品、业务领域和场景查看最新 AI Feature 信息：

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog

同时，关于如何在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中激活 SAP Business AI 功能，可参考 SAP Note 3651017。

总结

SAP 新一代 Assistant 与 Agent 的发布，标志着 SAP Business AI 正从单点辅助能力进一步走向面向业务流程的代理式 AI。

Assistant 负责理解用户意图和协调工作，Agent 负责完成专业任务，Skill 负责执行确定性动作。整个过程运行在 SAP 的业务数据、标准流程、权限和治理框架内。

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户而言，这一方向并不是简单增加一个聊天入口，而是推动企业从应用驱动的操作方式，逐步走向以业务目标为中心的智能流程协同。

随着更多 Assistant、Agent 和 AI Feature 持续发布，SAP Cloud ERP 将进一步帮助企业构建更加智能、敏捷和可持续演进的运营核心。

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Serial Number while doing OBD - PGI
entry.content:
Hi Vamankumar,

Did you set the stock check to error in the serial number profile (transaction OIS2)? Then you'd get get an error message if you issue the same material/serial number combination a second time.

There's more info on this in the following note: https://me.sap.com/notes/612132

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: PFCG Role Generation After S/4HANA Upgrade: New T-Cod...
entry.content:
Will the role load only the new objects and fields related to the newly added T-code A, or will it also load the new objects and fields for all T-codes already assigned to the role?
When execute the update it will load (Add/delete/change) all need object, not only the new transaction that you added.
It is the same action as SU25 2C (Got role list), then merge in PFCG. In effect, the role will be synchronized with the current repository, which can introduce required objects, update existing entries, and remove items that are no longer aligned.

Best regards,
SAP Support
Xuejiao

entry.title: Re: 原価センタを無効化する手順と、無効にした際の影響について
entry.content:
Hello Sir,

To clarify — OKEON is for maintaining the Cost Center Standard Hierarchy, not for deactivating individual cost centers. For deactivation, please use KS02 and set the Valid To date.

Regarding the impact of changes made in OKEON, here is what you need to be aware of:

Reporting Any change in the hierarchy will take effect immediately and will also affect historical periods. All reports using the standard hierarchy (e.g., S_ALR_87013611, Fiori analytics) will reflect the new structure right away. Please inform your business users before proceeding.

Allocation Cycles If your assessment (KSV5) or distribution cycles (KSU5) reference the affected hierarchy nodes, moving cost centers between nodes may change your allocation results. Please review these before making any changes, especially near period-end.

Mid-Year Restrictions SAP will block organizational reassignments (company code, business area, profit center) on a cost center mid-year if:

  • Actual postings already exist in the current fiscal year
  • The cost center is linked to a Fixed Asset, Work Center, or HR master record

For further reference, please see the following SAP documentation:

  • Cost Centers and the Standard Hierarchy — SAP Help Portal
  • Deactivation of finance master data objects in S/4... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Unable to return valid error msg from SO enh to BAPI/...
entry.content:
Read first a note such as 2925296 - Missing messages from USEREXIT_SAVE_DOCUMENT_PREPARE (for information, not to be implemented if your version is not very old)

  1. update messages to the error log VBFS, xvbfs[]
  2. Those messages should be copied to RETURN parameter of BAPI

NB: EXIT_SAPMV45A_003 doesn't handle any error code, its purpose seems to be handling of copy requirements At Header Level.
